title: Immunocytochemistry of motor neurons derived from iPSCs with the hNIL construct protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d683g","text":"This protocol describes the immunocytochemistry for staining motor neurons derived from induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) using the hNIL transgenic factors in a CLYBL safe harbor site. For the protocol on this differentiation, refer to the Clelland Lab’s Differentiation of iPSCs with the hNIL construct into motor neurons protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: This protocol can be followed at any time after Day 7 in the hNIL differentiation of iPSCs into motor neurons protocol. This protocol has been optimized to neurons plated on 96-well plates. Without removing media from the wells, add 4% PFA on all target wells and let it sit for 00:30:00 at Room temperature (the volume of 4% PFA should equal the volume of media already in the well, resulting in a final well concentration of 2% PFA).

Discard the media and PFA by carefully and gently tapping the plate upside down onto absorbent wipes (KIMTECH Kimwipes are appropriate). Discard the Kimwipes in designated PFA waste. If fixing and staining need to be performed on separate days, after step 2, wash the plate thrice with 1X DPBS, 00:10:00 each time at slow agitation on the Belly Dancer. Then store the plate at 4 °C , with the sides covered by parafilm to avoid evaporation. Only permeabilize the cells (step 3) on the day that the cells will be incubated with primary antibody.
Wash cells with 1X DPBS + 0.1% Triton-X (“DPBS-T” at 100 µL per well) to permeabilize the cells.
Block the target wells with 1X DPBS-T + 5% BSA at Room temperature for 01:00:00 ( 50 µL /well). Do not exceed this time to avoid over blocking your neurons.

Discard blocking agent onto an absorbent wipe, then add primary antibodies diluted in 1X DPBS-T + 5% BSA ( 50 µL /well) at the appropriate concentrations. It is advisable to include the beta tubulin monoclonal primary antibody (1:250 from ThermoFisher Scientific Catalog # 480011) to show neuronal morphology.
Incubate at 4 °C Overnight on a plate shaker, with the sides covered by parafilm to avoid evaporation.
Briefly wash cells with 1X DPBS + 0.1% Triton-X (“DPBS-T” at 100 µL per well). Discard the spent wash onto absorbent wipes. (1/3)
Wash cells with 1X DPBS + 0.1% Triton-X (“DPBS-T” at 100 µL per well) for 00:10:00 at slow agitation on the Belly Dancer. Discard the spent wash onto absorbent wipes. (2/3)
Wash cells with 1X DPBS + 0.1% Triton-X (“DPBS-T” at 100 µL per well) for 00:10:00 at slow agitation on the Belly Dancer. Discard the spent wash onto absorbent wipes. (3/3)
Discard the primary antibody solution from each well using a multichannel pipette. Ensure to never touch the bottom of the well and always pipette slowly to not lift neurons from the well bottom.
Wash with 1X DPBS-T to remove any unbound primary antibody ( 100 µL /well). First briefly, then twice for 10 minutes each time at slow agitation on the Belly Dancer.
Add desired secondary antibodies diluted in 1X DPBS-T + 5% BSA. Incubate at Room temperature for 01:00:00 in the belly dancer.
Wash once briefly with 1X DPBS-T ( 100 µL /well).
Wash with 1X DPBS-T with DAPI (1:1000-1:10,000) for 00:10:00 (100 μL/well).
Wash with 1X DPBS (100 μL/well).
Change the media to 1X DPBS or 1X DPBS + Sodium Azide 0.02%, if storing long term, ( 100 µL /well) and store at 4 °C .
Wash with 1X DPBS-T briefly. (1/3)
Wash with 1X DPBS-T for 00:10:00 at slow agitation on the Belly Dancer. (2/3)
Wash with 1X DPBS-T for 00:10:00 at slow agitation on the Belly Dancer. (3/3)
Wash with 1X DPBS for 00:10:00 . (1/2)
Wash with 1X DPBS for 00:10:00 . (2/2)
creator: Maria Sckaff
